title: Brush and Floss Your Teeth
summary: Build a simple daily routine to keep your teeth and gums clean and healthy with proper brushing and flossing.
learnerPromise: Keep your teeth and gums healthy with a simple daily routine.
whyItMatters: Brushing and flossing remove food and plaque that cause cavities and gum problems. A daily routine helps prevent tooth pain and expensive dental visits.
realLifeUses: Cleaning teeth every morning and night
Removing food stuck between teeth after meals
Preventing bad breath throughout the day
Keeping gums from bleeding or getting swollen
youWillLearn: How to brush each part of your mouth for the right amount of time
How to floss between teeth without hurting your gums
How often to brush and floss each day
miniChallenge: Brush and floss once, paying attention to each tooth surface.
steps: Wet your toothbrush and apply a pea-sized amount of toothpaste.
Brush the outer, inner, and chewing surfaces of all teeth for about two minutes total.
Use gentle circular motions and do not press too hard.
Take a piece of floss about the length of your forearm and wrap it around your fingers.
Slide the floss gently between each tooth and curve it around the tooth base to clean below the gum line.
completionCriteria: You brushed all tooth surfaces for about two minutes
You flossed between your teeth gently
You did not press so hard that your gums hurt or bled
commonProblems: Your gums bleed when flossing — this often improves after a few days of regular flossing
You are not sure you brushed long enough — use a timer or a song
Floss feels hard to use — try floss picks or a water flosser as an alternative
tips: Brush for two minutes, twice a day
Replace your toothbrush about every three months or when the bristles look worn
Wait about 30 minutes after eating acidic foods before brushing
helpBoundary: Ask a dentist if your gums bleed regularly or you have persistent tooth pain.
